name: routing_per_tenant_v1
description: Per-tenant routing isolation and configuration dataset for the adapterOS K-sparse LoRA router. Covers tenant-specific routing parameters (K, tau, epsilon), adapter pool composition with shared system adapters, cross-tenant isolation guarantees, shared adapter divergent routing outcomes, and per-tenant optimization strategies. All examples assume Q15-quantized gate scores and deterministic top-K selection.

entries.notes: 2000 examples across 5 subcategories: tenant_routing_config (400), adapter_pool_composition (400), cross_tenant_isolation (400), shared_adapter_routing (400), routing_optimization (400)

provenance.review_notes: Synthetic dataset covering per-tenant routing isolation, adapter pool composition with system adapters, tenant-specific K/tau/epsilon tuning, cross-tenant independence guarantees, and optimization strategies for diverse tenant workloads.

evaluation_gates: Tenant routing pools include only owned adapters plus system adapters
Cross-tenant adapter pools never leak dedicated adapters between tenants
Tenant-specific K value controls the sparsity of adapter selection independently
Tenant-specific temperature tau reshapes the softmax distribution per-tenant
Tenant-specific entropy floor epsilon prevents routing collapse per-tenant
System adapters compete fairly in every tenant's gate scoring
Identical system adapter produces different routing outcomes across tenants
Routing decisions for tenant-a are fully independent of tenant-b's state
intent: Train the adapter to reason about per-tenant routing isolation in a multi-tenant adapterOS deployment: how each tenant's adapter pool is constructed, how tenant-specific K/tau/epsilon parameters shape routing behavior, how system adapters participate across all tenants with independent outcomes, and how to tune routing parameters for different tenant workload profiles.

subcategories.tenant_routing_config: How each tenant customizes K, tau, epsilon. Impact on routing behavior. When to use K=1 vs K=4.
subcategories.adapter_pool_composition: Tenant has N custom adapters + M system adapters = total pool. How system adapters interact with tenant adapters in the gate.
subcategories.cross_tenant_isolation: Tenant-a's routing decisions do not affect tenant-b. Gate weights are independent per tenant. No state leakage.
subcategories.shared_adapter_routing: System adapter gets high gate score for one tenant but low for another. Same shared adapter, different routing outcomes.
subcategories.routing_optimization: Tuning tau and epsilon for different workloads. High-precision tenant (low tau) vs exploration tenant (high tau).
